text: This study aims to investigate the strategy used by teachers of Islamic Religious Education (PAI) at SDN 2 Kesumadadi to cultivate religious character of elementary school students. This study is motivated by the necessity of building religious character from an early age, and it aims to solve the problems faced by the school, namely the low ability of elementary school students in reading the Quran. This study uses qualitative field research method with data collection through depth interviews, field observation, and documentation analysis. The results of the study show that the strategy used by PAI teachers is by habituating students in reading Asma' al-Husna. Although this habituation is carried out in the context of Islamic Religious Education lessons (PAI) and is not performed as a daily morning routine, it is very effective in nurturing the students' religious character. The teacher uses various strategies to habituate students in reading Asma' al-Husna, namely by illustrating and applying the norms of religion, by giving guidance continuously, by giving appreciation to students, and through discipline in order to maintain the students' behaviour. The result shows that the strategy used by the teachers to cultivate religious character of students through habituation of reading Asma' al-Husna can contribute to increasing the students' religious knowledge and ability to memorise and to cultivate the students' overall character, namely discipline, good communication skill, and interest in learning. This study is expected to be useful in understanding the importance of building religious character at school by arranging the students' religious activities systematically so as to establish a strong religious character for elementary school students.
